stimulus:
[Table: Effects of Rhizobium Bacteria on 3 Legume Species. Columns: plant species, rhizobium host (yes/no), average mass with rhizobium present (g), average mass with rhizobium absent (g). Rows: soybean—yes, 22.4, 14.1; chickpea—yes, 18.7, 13.5; lentil—no, 9.2, 9.1.]

Rhizobium bacteria form symbiotic relationships with the roots of many legumes, fixing atmospheric nitrogen into a form the plant can use. Plants that benefit from this relationship are called rhizobium hosts. A botanist studying three legumes wanted to determine the extent to which growth depends on rhizobium presence and concluded that, of the three species, ______blank
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the statement?

answerOptions.content: chickpea grown in soil containing rhizobium had a higher average mass than soybean grown in soil containing rhizobium.
lentil had the highest average mass of all three species grown in soil that had been treated to kill rhizobium, while soybean had the lowest.
soybean grown in soil containing rhizobium had a slightly higher average mass than soybean grown in soil that had been treated to kill rhizobium.
soybean grown in soil containing rhizobium had a much higher average mass than soybean grown in soil that had been treated to kill rhizobium.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it uses the table data most effectively to convey the central finding. Soybean is a rhizobium host, and according to the table, soybean grown with rhizobium had an average mass of 22.4 g versus 14.1 g without rhizobium—a much higher average mass, consistent with the host relationship.

Choice A is incorrect because the table shows soybean (22.4 g) exceeded chickpea (18.7 g) when both were grown with rhizobium, so chickpea did not have the higher average mass. Choice B is incorrect because the table shows soybean had the highest, not the lowest, average mass when grown without rhizobium (14.1 g), exceeding chickpea (13.5 g) and lentil (9.1 g). Choice C is incorrect because the difference between soybean grown with and without rhizobium is large, not slight.
